entity_name: Top 19 Automotive Parts Manufacturing Companies in Georgia
facts: Georgia's automotive parts manufacturing industry is an intricate system of companies specializing in producing vital components for vehicles.
The sector features enterprises ranging from family-owned businesses to large corporations crafting precision turned parts to environmentally friendly plastic components.
With rapid growth of electric and hybrid vehicles, manufacturers are pivoting towards sustainable practices and advanced technologies.
Many firms are responding to heightened demand for innovative automotive solutions.
The list includes 19 automotive parts manufacturing companies based in Georgia.
Firms range from small private operations to larger entities employing hundreds to thousands of workers.
Companies were founded between the 1960s and 2023.
Specializations include stamped parts, suspension components, and recycled automotive parts.
Locations span from Lavonia to Atlanta.
Haering Precision USA LP is a subsidiary of Anton Häring KG and a family-owned precision parts manufacturer based in Lavonia, Georgia.
Haering Precision USA LP was founded in 1961 and employs around 288 workers.
The company specializes in high-quality precision turned parts for traditional combustion engines, hybrid and electric vehicles, and hydrogen fuel cells.
Haering Precision USA LP received the Global Supplier Award from Bosch and the Premium Supplier Award from Schaeffler.
Jefferson Southern Corporation is based in Rockmart, Georgia, established in 2001 with a headcount of 201-500.
The company focuses on high-quality stamped and welded components for vehicle structural integrity and performance.
Jefferson Southern Corporation operates as a Tier One supplier providing parts ready for installation to the vehicle's interior shell or 'white body'.
Ajin Georgia is based in Register, Georgia, founded in 2023 with a headcount of 501-1000.
The company specializes in OEM parts for vehicle production and supports Hyundai Motor Group Metaplant America.
Ajin Georgia made a recent $40,000 donation to local organizations.
F&P Georgia is based in Rome, Georgia, established in 2000 with a headcount of 201-500.
The company specializes in suspension components including subframes, lower arms, and pedals.
F&P Georgia operates as a tier one supplier under the Ftech Inc. network.
Sewon America Inc. is based in Rincon, Georgia, established in 1985 with over 1000 employees.
The company specializes in automotive stamping and interior parts while investing in R&D for manufacturing processes.
Trenton Pressing is based in Trenton, Georgia, established in 1995 with a headcount of 201-500.
The company operates from over 192,000 square feet across three facilities and serves as a direct Tier One supplier to BMW and Club Car.
KTX America Corporation is based in Toccoa, Georgia, also known as NIFCO KTW America, established in 2016 with a headcount of 201-500.
The company received $47.6M in funding in April 2014 and specializes in plastic components for vehicle interiors and exteriors.
KTX America Corporation serves BMW and Daimler using multi-component injection molding and high-gloss painting.
Hyundai MOBIS Alabama, LLC - Georgia Plant is based in West Point, Georgia, established in 2007 with a headcount of 501-1000.
The facility assembles and supplies chassis modules, cockpit modules, and front-end modules to support KIA's manufacturing operations via just-in-sequence delivery.
Ecoplastic America Corporation is based in Register, Georgia, founded in 1966 with a headcount of 501-1000.
The company operates as a tier-1 supplier for Hyundai Motor Group producing bumpers, consoles, trims, and plastic molds.
Ecoplastic America Corporation is actively building a manufacturing facility in Bulloch County, Georgia, expected to create numerous jobs.
Honda Lock of America is based in Bremen, Georgia, also known as Minebea Access Solutions, founded in 1962 with approximately 207 employees.
The company specializes in automotive and machinery safety and security components for four-wheeled and two-wheeled vehicles.
Novelis is an aluminum manufacturer based in Atlanta, Georgia, founded in 2005 with over 10,000 employees.
Novelis is the largest recycler of aluminum worldwide and supplies aluminum sheet and lightweight battery enclosures for electric vehicles.
The company received $750.0M in debt funding in January 2025 and is a subsidiary of Hindalco Industries Limited.
Piolax is based in Canton, Georgia, founded in 1931 with around 396 employees.
The company specializes in plastic and metal fastening products including trim clips, fuel valves, and glove box handles.
Piolax received a $432,000 grant in March 2024.
FuelTech USA is based in Ball Ground, Georgia, founded in 2003 with around 21 employees.
The company specializes in automotive performance products including engine management systems, wiring solutions, and ignition systems for the motorsports industry.
Boles Parts Supply Inc is based in Atlanta, Georgia, founded in 1964 with a headcount of 11-50.
The company specializes in remanufactured components including electrical parts, A/C products, and brake components while offering reverse logistics services.
Boles Parts Supply Inc operates over 1,000,000 square feet of warehouse space.
Road Tested Parts is a family-owned company based in Carnesville, Georgia, founded in 2021 with a headcount of 201-500.
The company distributes recycled OEM automotive parts to over 50,000 repair shops throughout the Southeastern United States.
Road Tested Parts expanded through strategic acquisitions of established automotive recycling businesses.
ALBAform is based in Flowery Branch, Georgia, founded in 2011 with a headcount of 51-200.

The company specializes in custom metal fabrication for automotive and e-mobility sectors, producing interior parts for automotive seating such as seat cushion structures and dashboard reinforcements.
ALBAform operates facilities in the USA, Mexico, and Czech Republic and is a 100% women-owned business.
Auto Metal Direct is based in Gainesville, Georgia, founded in 2006 with a headcount of 51-200.
The company specializes in restoration parts for classic American cars and trucks including body panels, trim, and glass, and participates in the General Motors Restoration Parts Program.
Lawson Industries, Inc. is based in Albany, Georgia, founded in 1998 with 23 employees.
The company specializes in automotive exhaust systems and components including muffler clamps, performance exhausts, and catalytic converters while serving customers across 39 states.
